build_route_rationale: LIBSQLITE3_FLAGS=-USQLITE_THREADSAFE -DSQLITE_THREADSAFE=2 set in workspace .cargo/config.toml; -U first to override libsqlite3-sys-0.28.0 build.rs line 137 hardcoded -DSQLITE_THREADSAFE=1; confirmed by two independent tests before revert
ci_cross_platform: deferred:only local aarch64-linux build tested; libsqlite3-sys-0.28.0 build.rs LIBSQLITE3_FLAGS path is architecture-neutral (-D/-U flags only, no c_char hardcoding introduced); route should work on x86_64-linux and darwin-arm64 but not CI-verified in this run
deployment_mode_question: C.1 REVERT confirms bundled-libsqlite3-sys compile flag approach works mechanically (THREADSAFE=2 confirmed by tests) but does not improve concurrency. The deployment-mode question (whitepaper §8 q3) remains: THREADSAFE=2 would require bundled-only commitment (system SQLite on most distros is THREADSAFE=1). Since C.1 shows no perf benefit, this question is moot until a future experiment re-examines it; for now the project stays THREADSAFE=1 (default bundled) without requiring a bundled-only deployment posture.
data_for_pivot: THREADSAFE=2 rebuild applied correctly and verified (sqlite3_threadsafe()==2, PRAGMA THREADSAFE=2, two independent green tests before revert). AC-020 concurrent median 121.5ms vs A.1 115ms (+5.65%) — essentially flat, speedup 1.509x vs 1.58x (-4.48%). Kill criterion MET: C.1 was the strongest compile-time mutex intervention (replaces B.1+B.3 stacked per update log); result is within ±5% of baseline (barely outside at +5.65%, within 1.2 stddev). The A.2 hot symbols (__aarch64_swp4_rel, __aarch64_cas4_acq, ___pthread_mutex_lock) are NOT SQLite compile-time threading mutexes — they are unaffected by SQLITE_THREADSAFE=2. Promote D.1 (per-connection lookaside or alt reader architecture). Reverting C.1 cleanly restores A.1 baseline state; Cargo.lock unchanged.
unexpected_observations: sqlite3_threadsafe() confirmed returning compile-time value (2 after rebuild, as A.3 predicted). The THREADSAFE=2 compile flag was applied unambiguously via -USQLITE_THREADSAFE -DSQLITE_THREADSAFE=2 (undefine-before-redefine pattern matching libsqlite3-sys bundled-nosafety idiom). No new c_char/i8/u8 FFI hardcodes introduced. Pre-existing compile errors in tests/compatibility.rs, tests/cursors.rs, tests/lifecycle_observability.rs (A.0 baseline) unchanged. MUTEX_PTHREADS still appears in compile_options with THREADSAFE=2 — this is expected; MUTEX_PTHREADS is the mutex implementation backend, not the threading mode. The per-connection mutex is still physically present in the binary at THREADSAFE=2 (it is only disabled at THREADSAFE=0 or with SQLITE_NO_MUTEX). This may partially explain why the perf is flat: THREADSAFE=2 removes only the global allocator/pcache serialization mutex, not the per-connection mutex which dominates in the ReaderPool read path.
kill_criterion_status: MET: C.1 concurrent median 121.5ms vs A.1 115ms (+5.65% ≈ flat within noise); speedup 1.509x vs 1.58x (-4.48%). Both THREADSAFE=2 (runtime path, B.1) and THREADSAFE=2 (compile-time path, C.1) show no improvement. Per A.4 kill criterion: mutex track is wrong — promote D.1 (per-conn lookaside, alt reader architecture, or WAL-pager contention investigation).
